_comment_panel: BOE display backlight where dimming PWM comes from an i2c-tiny-usb-pwm Digispark dongle (not the Pi's GPIO+PWM hardware as in config_opti4001_boepwm.json). The dongle exposes a virtual I2C slave at 0x77 on the bus it offers (typically /dev/i2c-22 after USB enumeration); writing register 0x00 = 0..255 sets the duty cycle of its PB1 PWM output, which feeds the MPQ3367 LED driver's DIM input. The MPQ3367 itself is on the same bus at 0x38 - the daemon configures both at init. Pair with -DCONFIG_FILE=config_opti4001_boe_i2c_pwm.json at cmake configure time.
_comment_calibration: brightness_to_nits.sweep_table reuses the BOE PWM 2 kHz reference table from the Pi-driven boe_pwm setup. The brightness curve depends on the panel + MPQ3367 (which are unchanged), so this is a reasonable approximation despite the slightly different PWM frequency (1953 Hz here vs 2 kHz in the reference) and duty resolution (0..255 here vs ns-precision in the reference). Re-sweep with tools/als-dimmer-sweep.py for accuracy on your specific panel.
_comment_thermal_compensation: Disabled because there's no temperature sensor accessible to the daemon on this hardware path (MPQ3367 has no temperature output, the i2c-tiny-usb-pwm dongle only exposes the PWM control register). If you wire an external NTC or hwmon source later, configure i2c_temp_source or temp_command and flip enabled to true.
